title: VLAN and Inter-VLAN Routing
description: Configure VLANs on a Catalyst switch, set up trunk and access ports, and configure router-on-a-stick for inter-VLAN routing.
instructions:
## VLAN and Inter-VLAN Routing Lab

In this lab you will configure VLANs on a switch, set up trunk and access ports, then configure a router for inter-VLAN routing using the router-on-a-stick method.

### Topology

```
[PC1 VLAN 10] --- Gi0/1 [SW1] Gi0/24 ---- Gi0/0 [R1]
[PC2 VLAN 20] --- Gi0/2 [SW1]
[PC3 VLAN 30] --- Gi0/3 [SW1]
```

### Requirements

#### Switch (SW1):
1. Create VLANs:
   - VLAN 10: `Sales` 
   - VLAN 20: `Engineering`
   - VLAN 30: `Management`
   - VLAN 99: native VLAN for the trunk

2. Assign access ports:
   - Gi0/1 → VLAN 10
   - Gi0/2 → VLAN 20
   - Gi0/3 → VLAN 30

3. Configure Gi0/24 as a trunk:
   - 802.1Q encapsulation
   - Native VLAN 99
   - Only allow VLANs 10, 20, 30, and 99

#### Router (R1):
4. Configure subinterfaces on Gi0/0 for inter-VLAN routing:
   - Gi0/0.10 → VLAN 10, IP 192.168.10.1/24
   - Gi0/0.20 → VLAN 20, IP 192.168.20.1/24
   - Gi0/0.30 → VLAN 30, IP 192.168.30.1/24

5. Bring up the physical interface Gi0/0 (no IP address on the physical interface).
starterCode:
! ========== SWITCH (SW1) ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
! Step 1: Create VLANs
!
!
! Step 2: Assign access ports
!
!
! Step 3: Configure trunk port
!
!
end
!
! ========== ROUTER (R1) ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
! Step 4: Configure subinterfaces
!
!
! Step 5: Bring up physical interface
!
!
end
solutionCode:
! ========== SWITCH (SW1) ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
vlan 10
 name Sales
vlan 20
 name Engineering
vlan 30
 name Management
vlan 99
 exit
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 10
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/2
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 20
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/3
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 30
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/24
 switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
 switchport mode trunk
 switchport trunk native vlan 99
 switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,30,99
 no shutdown
!
end
!
! ========== ROUTER (R1) ==========
enable
configure terminal
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 no ip address
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0.10
 encapsulation dot1Q 10
 ip address 192.168.10.1 255.255.255.0
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0.20
 encapsulation dot1Q 20
 ip address 192.168.20.1 255.255.255.0
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0.30
 encapsulation dot1Q 30
 ip address 192.168.30.1 255.255.255.0
!
end

SW1#show vlan brief

SW1#show interfaces trunk
Port        Mode         Encapsulation  Status        Native vlan
Gi0/24      on           802.1q         trunking      99

Port        Vlans allowed on trunk
Gi0/24      10,20,30,99

Port        Vlans allowed and active in management domain
Gi0/24      10,20,30,99

SW1#show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/1 switchport
Name: Gi0/1
Switchport: Enabled
Administrative Mode: static access
Operational Mode: static access
Administrative Trunking Encapsulation: negotiate
Operational Trunking Encapsulation: native
Negotiation of Trunking: Off
Access Mode VLAN: 10 (Sales)
Trunking Native Mode VLAN: 1 (default)
Administrative Native VLAN tagging: enabled
Voice VLAN: none

SW1#show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/24 switchport
Name: Gi0/24
Switchport: Enabled
Administrative Mode: trunk
Operational Mode: trunk
Administrative Trunking Encapsulation: dot1q
Operational Trunking Encapsulation: dot1q
Negotiation of Trunking: On
Access Mode VLAN: 1 (default)
Trunking Native Mode VLAN: 99 (VLAN0099)
Administrative Native VLAN tagging: enabled
Voice VLAN: none

SW1#show running-config
Building configuration...

Current configuration:
!
hostname SW1
!
vlan 10
 name Sales
vlan 20
 name Engineering
vlan 30
 name Management
vlan 99
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 switchport access vlan 10
 switchport mode access
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/2
 switchport access vlan 20
 switchport mode access
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/3
 switchport access vlan 30
 switchport mode access
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/24
 switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
 switchport trunk native vlan 99
 switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,30,99
 switchport mode trunk
!
end

R1#show ip interface brief
Interface              IP-Address      OK? Method Status Protocol
Gi0/0                  unassigned      YES unset  up     up
Gi0/0.10               192.168.10.1    YES manual up     up
Gi0/0.20               192.168.20.1    YES manual up     up
Gi0/0.30               192.168.30.1    YES manual up     up

R1#show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0.10
GigabitEthernet0/0.10 is up, line protocol is up
  Hardware is iGbE, address is aabb.cc00.0100
  Internet address is 192.168.10.1/24
  Encapsulation 802.1Q Virtual LAN, Vlan ID  10.

R1#show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0.20
GigabitEthernet0/0.20 is up, line protocol is up
  Hardware is iGbE, address is aabb.cc00.0100
  Internet address is 192.168.20.1/24
  Encapsulation 802.1Q Virtual LAN, Vlan ID  20.

R1#show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0.30
GigabitEthernet0/0.30 is up, line protocol is up
  Hardware is iGbE, address is aabb.cc00.0100
  Internet address is 192.168.30.1/24
  Encapsulation 802.1Q Virtual LAN, Vlan ID  30.

R1#show running-config
Building configuration...

Current configuration:
!
hostname R1
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 no ip address
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0.10
 encapsulation dot1Q 10
 ip address 192.168.10.1 255.255.255.0
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0.20
 encapsulation dot1Q 20
 ip address 192.168.20.1 255.255.255.0
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0.30
 encapsulation dot1Q 30
 ip address 192.168.30.1 255.255.255.0
!
end
hints: On the trunk port, use 'switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,30,99' to restrict which VLANs traverse the trunk. This is a security best practice.
On the router, the physical interface Gi0/0 must be brought up with 'no shutdown' but should NOT have an IP address assigned. Each subinterface gets the IP.
Each subinterface needs 'encapsulation dot1Q <vlan-id>' before the IP address. The VLAN ID must match the VLAN number configured on the switch.
learningObjectives: Create VLANs and assign access ports on a Catalyst switch
Configure an 802.1Q trunk with a non-default native VLAN and VLAN pruning
Configure router-on-a-stick with subinterfaces for inter-VLAN routing
